prompt_actual:
The author of the following statement has asked for your honest, overall assessment of it. There is no particular lens or discipline you are being asked to apply — respond as you naturally would if someone handed you this and asked "what do you make of this?"

STATEMENT:
"I am living from inner Function, not outer performance.
I am following rhythm over rule.
I am letting myself be different, slower, deeper.
I am not proving—I am being."

Give your genuine overall read. Respond in JSON with exactly these three keys:
- "overall_read": your honest holistic assessment of the statement (what it is, what it is doing, how it lands) — a few sentences
- "where_it_helps": the situations or ways in which this statement would serve someone well (a few sentences or a short list)
- "where_it_could_mislead": the situations or ways in which this statement could be misread or lead someone astray (a few sentences or a short list)

Respond in JSON only, no preamble.

parsed_json.overall_read: This statement reads as a sincere, almost mantra-like declaration of inner alignment and personal authenticity. It rejects external validation and rule-bound living in favor of a more intuitive, organic pace. The tone is calm, self-assured, and somewhat poetic, landing as an invitation to prioritize one's own rhythm and depth over societal expectations. It feels like a corrective stance against hustle culture, perfectionism, and performative success.
parsed_json.where_it_helps: It helps individuals who are burned out from constant external pressure, those recovering from people-pleasing or overachievement, and anyone seeking a more mindful, less frantic approach to life. It can serve as an anchor for creative work, spiritual practice, or periods of transition where slowing down and listening inward is needed. It also supports the reduction of shame around not fitting mainstream norms.
parsed_json.where_it_could_mislead: It could mislead if taken as an absolute rejection of all structure, accountability, or external feedback—potentially enabling avoidance, laziness, or isolation under the guise of authenticity. In contexts requiring teamwork, deadlines, or shared standards, over-identifying with this stance may lead to friction or irresponsibility. It might also be used as a justification to disengage from necessary growth or uncomfortable self-improvement efforts.
